preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar affricate [dz] to the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] (gamma) is a form of backing and spirantization. This sound change involves moving the place of articulation from the alveolar ridge to the soft palate while maintaining voicing and loosening the closure into friction.

examples.note: Reflects stages in complex consonant shifts where dental affricates retracted into velar fricative positions.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics.
